focus: traceability: test adequacy: does the plugin test suite cover the default-export contract, fail-closed, injection, continuation gates?

title: Default-export-only regression guard is not automated
detail: The tests prove that a default export exists by importing default.__test, but they do not assert that mk-goal.js has no named exports. The coupled plugin contract says every plugin file must export a default factory, and the feature catalog records a prior live failure where multiple named exports broke OpenCode plugin loading. A future added named export could therefore leave the unit suite green while violating the loader contract.
claim: The plugin suite does not fully cover the default-export-only contract because it lacks a module-shape assertion that rejects named exports.
counterevidenceSought: Searched the goal plugin tests and coupled plugin docs for an automated Object.keys(module) or no-named-exports assertion; only manual/readme guidance and default.__test imports were present.
alternativeExplanation: The project may rely on live OpenCode smoke testing for this loader shape, but the focus here is automated plugin suite adequacy and the documented regression mode is cheap to pin in unit tests.
downgradeTrigger: Drop once the suite asserts the module export keys are exactly ["default"] or an equivalent plugin-loader smoke test fails on named exports.
suggestedFix: Add a module-shape test importing mk-goal.js and asserting Object.keys(pluginModule) is exactly ["default"], then assert the default is a function and carries the __test surface.

title: Command router behavior is only manually covered
detail: goal.md defines the user-facing routing grammar, but the automated tests start at plugin.tool.mk_goal.execute or executeGoalAction and never exercise the command markdown route selection. The manual playbook contains live /goal steps, so command drift, allowed-tool drift, and router-level failure text drift are not guarded by the plugin test suite.
claim: The plugin suite does not cover the /goal command-router contract even though the command is part of the integrated goal surface.
counterevidenceSought: Searched .opencode/plugins, .opencode/commands, .opencode/bin, and .opencode/scripts for goal.md or /goal command tests; found direct tool tests and manual playbook coverage but no automated command-router harness.
alternativeExplanation: Because OpenCode commands are markdown instructions, the team may accept manual live validation for command routing; that still leaves a traceability gap relative to automated plugin suite coverage.
downgradeTrigger: Drop once an automated command-router contract test or live command smoke pins empty/show/set/bare text/clear/complete/pause/failure routing.
suggestedFix: Add a command-router contract harness that feeds representative $ARGUMENTS cases and asserts the selected mk_goal or mk_goal_status call plus the expected STATUS envelope/failure text.

title: Tool-level fail-closed missing-session paths are not pinned
detail: The state helper rejects missing session ids and the tool layer converts errors into STATUS=FAIL envelopes, but tests only cover helper-level rejection and successful tool calls with a sessionID. Missing-session tool context is the externally relevant fail-closed path, and it is not asserted for mk_goal or mk_goal_status.
claim: The plugin suite does not prove the public tool handlers fail closed when ToolContext lacks a session id.
counterevidenceSought: Checked goal tests for missing-session ToolContext cases and no-file-created assertions; only helpers.setGoal(null, ...) covers the lower helper path.
alternativeExplanation: The helper rejection may catch most implementation regressions, but it would not catch wrapper changes that fail open, throw raw errors, or format failures incorrectly at the public tool boundary.
downgradeTrigger: Drop once mk_goal and mk_goal_status have negative ToolContext tests asserting STATUS=FAIL, code=MISSING_SESSION_ID, and no temp state files.
suggestedFix: Add tool-level negative tests for mk_goal.execute({ action: "set", objective: "x" }, {}), mk_goal_status.execute({}, {}), and blank/null session variants, with state directory contents checked after each call.

title: Injection tests cover presence, not adversarial sanitization
detail: mk-goal.js contains explicit prompt-injection hardening for active_goal markers, role labels, long fence markers, and ignore/disregard instruction phrases, and renderGoalInjection places sanitized objective/verifier text into the system block. The automated injection test only asserts the active marker appears, while supervisor tests cover secret redaction but not prompt-injection strings. The manual playbook names prompt injection leakage as a failure mode, so adversarial injection coverage is missing from the automated suite.
claim: The plugin suite does not adequately cover injection hardening because it lacks adversarial objective/evidence cases.
counterevidenceSought: Looked for tests containing active_goal markers, system/developer role labels, triple backticks, or ignore/disregard instruction phrases in objectives/evidence; none were present in the goal plugin tests.
alternativeExplanation: Secret redaction coverage proves one sanitization family works, but prompt-injection hardening is a separate contract with different regexes and output expectations.
downgradeTrigger: Drop once tests assert adversarial objective/evidence strings are redacted or neutralized in injection_preview and transformed system output.
suggestedFix: Add injection-focused tests using [active_goal], system:, developer:, triple backticks, and ignore previous instructions in objectives and verifier evidence, then assert raw strings do not survive in renderGoalInjection or the transform output.

title: Continuation gate tests cover the main ladder but not several fail-closed gates
detail: maybeContinueGoal has gates for missing session, active goal presence, suppressed goals, in-flight continuations, permission/question blocks, busy/retry runtime state, cooldown, wall/time caps, budget exhaustion, missing promptAsync, smoke mode, active dispatch, and promptAsync failure cleanup. The continuation test covers default disabled, passive, smoke, active success, and auto-turn cap, but not many fail-closed gates or rejected promptAsync cleanup.
claim: The continuation tests do not adequately cover the full fail-closed gate ladder that controls autonomous prompt submission.
counterevidenceSought: Checked mk-goal-continuation.test.cjs for assertions on missing_session_id, goal_not_active, continuation_in_flight, permission_or_question_block, session_busy, session_retry, cooldown, budget_exhausted, prompt_async_unavailable, and prompt_async_failed; those gate reasons are not exercised.
alternativeExplanation: The current tests hit the highest-level autonomy modes and cap behavior, which is valuable smoke coverage, but they do not pin the fail-closed cases most likely to prevent unsafe continuation.
downgradeTrigger: Drop once table-driven continuation tests cover every gate reason and verify locks/counters after promptAsync rejection.
suggestedFix: Extend mk-goal-continuation.test.cjs with negative cases for each gate reason plus a rejected-promptAsync case that verifies inFlightContinuations is cleared and immediate retry is suppressed by cooldown/counter state rather than a leaked lock.
